2604.18373.title: Dissecting AI Trading: Behavioral Finance and Market Bubbles
2604.18373.paper_summary: We study how AI agents form expectations and trade in experimental asset markets. Using a simulated open-call auction populated by autonomous Large Language Model (LLM) agents, we document three main findings. First, AI agents exhibit classic behavioral patterns: a pronounced disposition effect and recency-weighted extrapolative beliefs. Second, these individual-level patterns aggregate into equilibrium dynamics that replicate classic experimental findings (Smith et al., 1988), including the predictive power of excess demand for future prices and the positive relationship between disagreement and trading volume. Third, by analyzing the agents' reasoning text through a twenty-mechanism scoring framework, we show that targeted prompt interventions causally amplify or suppress specific behavioral mechanisms, significantly altering the magnitude of market bubbles.
2604.18373.paper_summary_zh: 我們研究AI代理如何形成期望並在實驗資產市場中進行交易。<lb/>通過一個由自主大型語言模型（LLM）代理構成的模擬公開拍賣，我們記錄了三個主要發現。<lb/>首先，AI代理表現出經典的行為模式：明顯的處置效應和近期加權的外推信念。<lb/>第二，這些個體層面的模式聚合成平衡動態，重現經典實驗發現（Smith et al., 1988），包括過度需求對未來價格的預測能力以及分歧與交易量之間的正相關關係。<lb/>第三，通過分析代理的推理文本，使用二十種機制評分框架，我們展示了有針對性的提示干預在因果上放大或抑制特定行為機制，顯著改變市場泡沫的規模。
2604.18373.author: Shumiao Ouyang et.al.
